title: LIFTING POINTS AND TOW TRUCK TOWING

figures_detected.description: Underside view of vehicle showing 2-pole lift points, sill locations, lift pad detail, center of gravity, and 850mm dimension between lift points

content_blocks.text: LIFTING POINTS AND TOW TRUCK TOWING
When lifting the vehicle, open the lift arms as wide as possible and ensure that the front and rear of the vehicle are well balanced.
When setting the lift arm, do not allow the arm to contact the brake tubes and fuel lines.

content_blocks.description: Underside view of vehicle showing 2-pole lift points with 850mm spacing, sill, lift pad, and center of gravity

callouts.label: 850mm
callouts.position: dimension between front and rear lift point pairs

callouts.label: Sill
callouts.position: lower centre of diagram, callout to sill flange

callouts.label: Lift pad
callouts.position: lower centre of diagram, callout to lift pad detail

content_blocks.text: Note. Lift-up points are the same as pantograph jack points.
Put the sill in the slit of the lift pad to prevent the sill from deforming. If the pad does not have a slit, prepare a suitable attachment with one.
